text.title: Lemon Squeezy Ops Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in indie LS operations. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: EU buyer reports being charged VAT twice — once via LS checkout, once on the founder's separate invoice. Refund request + complaint.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder kept the pre-LS invoicing flow active even after enabling LS as Merchant of Record.
antipattern.fix: Disable parallel VAT invoice for LS-source orders. LS receipt is the legal invoice. Refund duplicate VAT; apology email. Rule r2.

antipattern.symptom: Subscription software pirated days after launch; same hard-coded key in cracked builds.
antipattern.root-cause: License key embedded in the binary; no online validation.
antipattern.fix: Enable LS License API per variant; validate at startup + daily cadence; revoke compromised keys on refund. Rule r3.

antipattern.symptom: Monthly involuntary churn spike — failed renewals not recovered, MRR drops despite no voluntary cancels.
antipattern.root-cause: Only T+1 reminder configured; no T+4 / T+8 follow-up.
antipattern.fix: Wire 3-email sequence per templates/dunning-sequence.md. Expect 35-45% recovery on failed cards. Rule r4.

antipattern.symptom: Affiliates quit the program within 30 days; commission tracker shows clicks but few conversions per affiliate.
antipattern.root-cause: commission_pct set below 30% — affiliates view the program as not worth promoting.
antipattern.fix: Raise commission to 35-45% (within r5 band). Re-invite affiliates with updated terms. Document rationale if band is intentionally exceeded.
